mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real GDP above 3.5% signals a strengthening demand environment that increases corporate profitability and reduces equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in the CPI above 3% signals a dovish monetary policy stance that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows and increases inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in dovish periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% and a 2-month decline in initial claims below 200k signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in housing starts above 1.3m signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real GDP above 3.5% and a 2-month rise in real disposable personal income above 4% signals a strengthening demand environment that increases corporate profitability and reduces equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
